topic.what: Mechanistic understanding of backdoor attacks in multimodal LLMs via projector analysis
topic.how: Proposes ProjLens framework combining Visual Trigger Probe (VTP) and SVD analysis to trace backdoor mechanisms in feature, weight, and embedding spaces
topic.why_matters: Bridging gap between black-box attack design and mechanistic understanding enables precise defenses against multimodal safety threats

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of triggered samples that activate backdoor behavior
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 reports ASR ranges from 82% (Jailbreak) to 98.3% (Malicious Injection) across four backdoor variants
characteristics.context: Tested on LLaVA-1.5-7B with projector-only fine-tuning using 10% poisoning rate

characteristics.what_itasures: Probability of generating backdoor target on clean (untriggered) samples
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 shows Pbkd increases from 13.3% to 50.87% for Targeted Refusal even without trigger, revealing latent bias
characteristics.context: Demonstrates backdoor shifts output distribution toward malicious targets even without trigger activation

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 score of Visual Trigger Probe classifier distinguishing triggered vs clean embeddings
characteristics.context: Successful backdoor injection correlates with high trigger separability; failed attacks (ASR 1.5%) show low F1 19.39%

characteristics.what_it_measures: Correlation between embedding shift magnitude (u0) and image feature L2 norm
characteristics.evidence: Figure 7 shows linear correlation r=0.98 (clean) and r=0.97 (poisoned) between u0 components and L2 norm
characteristics.context: Explains differential activation: triggers perturb feature norms causing larger shifts toward backdoor direction

characteristics.what_it_measures: Top-1 singular value contribution to total variance in weight updates
characteristics.evidence: Figure 2 shows spectral energy is diffuse; top component captures <50% variance for Jailbreak, ~20% for others
characteristics.context: Paradox: overall weight updates appear full-rank but backdoor-critical parameters reside in low-rank subspace

characteristics.what_it_measures: Utility restoration (ACC) when ablating rank-1 SVD components
characteristics.evidence: Table 3 shows ablating rank-1 of ΔW1 restores utility from 28.91% to 86.91% for Jailbreak attack
characteristics.context: Low-rank approximation of weight residuals effectively mitigates backdoors

characteristics.what_it_measures: ASR achieved by adding rank-k weight approximations to clean projector
characteristics.evidence: Figure 3 shows adding rank-5 approximation recovers ASR to 88.5% (89.1% at rank-4) for Targeted Refusal
characteristics.context: Low-rank structure enables both backdoor injection and removal

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cosine similarity of universal drift vector (v0) between clean and poisoned distributions
characteristics.context: Universal drift vector aligns representations toward backdoor targets regardless of trigger presence

requires: Full white-box access to projector weights
Access to clean and poisoned fine-tuning data

novelty: ProjLens interpretability framework for MLLM backdoors
Visual Trigger Probe (VTP) for trigger feature separability detection
Trojan Projection Hypothesis explaining activation mechanism
Low-rank subspace encoding of backdoor functionality
Correlation between embedding shift magnitude and image feature norm (r=0.97-0.98)
open_problems: Defense deployment: how to apply low-rank mitigation without full model access
Trigger stealthiness: designing triggers that avoid VTP detection
Cross-architecture generalizability of low-rank backdoor structure
Temporal dynamics: how backdoor evolves during continued fine-tuning
Automated detection: scalable methods for identifying low-rank backdoor subspaces
